summary: This week in AI news, OpenAI and Meta are making significant moves in the industry. OpenAI is expanding its reach into households and launching new models, while Meta is removing controversial AI features and entering the AI coding battle. Meanwhile, Apple is suing OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft, and Google is now disclosing which ads are made with AI.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is raising concerns about accountability, transparency, and ethics, highlighting the need for responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults.
Meta is turning off the Instagram feature that let users generate AI images based on public accounts.
Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance OpenAI's hardware plans.

articles.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets

articles.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue

articles.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
articles.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

articles.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
articles.description: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The

articles.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
articles.description: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

articles.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
articles.description: About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has

articles.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
articles.description: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

articles.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
articles.description: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.

articles.title: Our approach to government and national security partnerships
articles.description: Learn how OpenAI approaches government and national security partnerships, with principles for responsible AI use, democratic accountability, and public safety.
